rationale: Major frictions across the board: this is a high-energy male-skewing UGC stunt hook that explicitly references "Tetris Block Party," not Marble Sort, and the palette (bold purple/gold/white) and adrenaline tone clash hard with the game's calm, pastel, satisfying-sort identity. The backflip-onto-bed gag does nothing to communicate the physics-tap color-sorting loop and would mis-target the 25-55 female casual puzzle audience who came for relaxation. Skip this archetype for Marble Sort; if testing UGC at all, swap to a calm "oddly satisfying" POV showing the conveyor sort itself.
